question: Chapters
feedback: The text does not clearly list the main sections of the thesis. While it includes a table with a timeline and some activities, it lacks a structured outline of chapters or major sections such as Introduction, Literature Review, Methodology, Analysis, Conclusion, etc. The table appears to be a work plan rather than a chapter list.

question: Description
feedback: The text does not provide a brief description of the contents of each section. It focuses on the problem statement, relevance, and work plan, but does not outline what each chapter will cover. There is no indication of what will be discussed in the literature review, methodology, comparative analysis, or conclusion sections.

question: Structure
feedback: It is not easy to understand the general structure of the thesis from this text. The content is dominated by background information, motivation, and a work plan, but lacks a clear hierarchical or logical presentation of the thesis chapters and their relationships. The reader cannot infer the flow or organization of the full thesis based on this section alone.
